[[commons:Category:Gassan-jinja]][[en:Gassan Shrine]][[es:Santuario Gassan]][[fr:Gassan-jinja]][[ja:月山神社]][[simple:Gassan Shrine]][[zh:月山神社]] {{Expand Japanese|月山神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Gassan Shrine | native_name = 月山神社 | image = Gassan jinja.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Gassan Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|38.548695|140.026962}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ar|تسوكويومي|az|Tsukuyomi|bcl|Tsukuyomi|ca|Tsukuyomi|cs|Cukujomi|da|Tsuki-yomi|de|Tsukuyomi|el|Τσουκουγιόμι|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|eo|Cukujomi|es|Tsukuyomi|eu|Tsukuyomi|fa|تسوکویومی|fi|Tsukuyomi|fr|Tsukuyomi|hr|Tsukuyomi|hu|Cukujomi|hy|Ցուկույոմի-նո-Միկոտո|id|Tsukuyomi|it|Tsukuyomi|ja|ツクヨミ|ko|츠쿠요미|mk|Цукујоми|mni|ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯌꯣꯃꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|no|Tsukuyomi|pl|Tsukuyomi|pt|Tsukuyomi|ru|Цукиёми|sh|Tsukuyomi|simple|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|sl|Cukijomi|sr|Цукујоми|su|Tsukuyomi|sv|Tsukuyomi|ta|சுக்குயோமி|th|สึกูโยมิ|tr|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|uk|Цукуйомі|vi|Tsukuyomi|zh_min_nan|Tukuyomi|zh|月讀|qid=Q595520|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | established = {'time': '+0593-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} | website = {{Official website|http://www.dewasanzan.jp/publics/index/51/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Gassan Shrine'''|月山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Dewa Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q1656379|ltq=Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} ** P1001: Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1348827|ltq=Q1348827|qid=Q1348827|1=Shōnai, Yamagata}} * {{ill|Q876032|ltq=Q876032|qid=Q876032|1=Dewa Province}} * Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * がっさんじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 308774928 == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 1425438 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 001170942 == Commons category (P373) == * Gassan-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Shrine|lt=Tsukiyama Shrine|qid=Q135040053|1=Tsukiyama Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Co-Enshrinement at Gassan Shrine|lt=Co-Enshrinement at Gassan Shrine|qid=Q135069866|1=Co-Enshrinement at Gassan Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山形県東田川郡庄内町立谷澤字本澤31', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山形県飽海郡遊佐町', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/011q4k3n == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 月山神社 == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q3174863|ltq=Q3174863|qid=Q3174863|1=Mount Gassan}} {{commons category|Category:Gassan-jinja}} [[EN:Gassan Shrine]] [[ES:Santuario Gassan]] [[FR:Gassan-jinja]] [[JA:月山神社]] [[SIMPLE:Gassan Shrine]] [[ZH:月山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11516052}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|{{ill|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|Q125863|qid=Q125863|1=Yamagata Prefecture}} shrine|shrines with the same name|Gassan Shrine (disambiguation)} {{神社 |名称 = Gassan Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Gassan jinja.JPG|270px|Gassan Shrine, full view]]
Full view; the main sanctuary is at the very back
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|38|32|48.7|N|140|1|32.8|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|Q125863|qid=Q125863|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Higashitagawa District|Q1155781|qid=Q1155781|1=Higashitagawa District, Yamagata}}{{ill|lt=Shōnai, Yamagata|Q1348827|qid=Q1348827|1=Shōnai, Yamagata}} Tachiyazawa {{ill|lt=aza|Q5327509|qid=Q5327509|lt=Aza|1=koaza}} Honsawa 31 | 緯度度 = 38 | 緯度分 = 32 | 緯度秒 = 48.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 1 | 経度秒 = 32.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Yamagata |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system#Government shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kanpei Taisha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) 1st year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=593|Q33889|qid=Q33889|1=593}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Yosemune-zukuri|Q162034|qid=Q162034|1=Hip roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = {{ill|lt=Dewa Sanzan|Q1155726|qid=Q1155726|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=August 14|Q2775|qid=Q2775|1=August 14}} |神事 = }} '''Gassan Shrine''' (がっさんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Gassan|Q3174863|qid=Q3174863|1=Mount Gassan}} (elevation 1,984 {{ill|lt=metre|Q11573|qid=Q11573|lt=m|1=Metre}}) in {{ill|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|Q125863|qid=Q125863|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. It is a Shikinaisha listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'; under the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was the only {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system#Government shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kanpei Taisha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} in the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku region|Q129465|qid=Q129465|1=Tōhoku region}}. Since ancient times it has been a site of {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|qid=Q93697439|1=Mountain worship}} centered on {{ill|lt=Shugendō|Q1483875|qid=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}}, and still draws many practitioners and worshippers. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} Through {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, the {{ill|lt=honji suijaku|Q3080781|qid=Q3080781|lt=original Buddhist ground deity|1=Honji suijaku}} of the Gassan deity came to be regarded as {{ill|lt=Amida Nyorai|Q236242|qid=Q236242|1=Amitābha}}. It can be said to be a Tōhoku characteristic that Amida Nyorai—also the honji-butsu of {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}—became the honji-butsu of the Gassan deity identified with Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the spread of {{ill|lt=Pure Land Buddhism|Q262244|qid=Q262244|1=Pure Land Buddhism}} brought Amida worship to GassanEdited by Ken'ichi Tanigawa, 'Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Sites 12, Tōhoku and Hokkaidō' (Hakusuisha, June 1984). Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, the deity of Gassan was considered Hachiman DaibosatsuYasuaki Togawa, 'Dewa Sanzan: History and Culture' (Ikubundō, August 1973). Also, Gassan’s auspicious year is said to be the {{ill|lt=Rabbit|Q16662792|qid=Q16662792|lt=Year of the Rabbit|1=mǎo}}, and visiting in Rabbit years is said to increase blessings. === Origin and history === [[ファイル:Gassan.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sacred peak|1=UNKNOWN}} Gassan]] [[ファイル:Gassan honguu torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Wooden torii at Midagahara]] [[ファイル:Gassan jinja Gate.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Stone torii
Inside the stone torii and stone wall is sacred precinct, so photography is prohibited there. A stone marker for Kanpei Taisha can be seen beyond the torii.]] [[ファイル:Precincts of Gassan jinja.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Distant view of the shrine
Mount Chōkai can be seen in the left background.]] According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Prince Hachiko|Q7244045|qid=Q7244045|1=Prince Hachiko}}, the third prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sushun|Q349238|qid=Q349238|1=Emperor Sushun}}, opened {{ill|lt=Mount Haguro|Q3090765|qid=Q3090765|lt=Haguro-san|1=Mount Haguro}} in the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=593|Q33889|qid=Q33889|1=593}}), and in the same year opened Mount Gassan and established this shrine. Prince Hachiko is said to have cared deeply for local people and listened to their worries and suffering, and thus came to be called “Nojo-sen”Also called “Nojo Taishi” or “Nojo Daishi.”. However, as far as historical sources indicate, there is no evidence that the founder Nojo-sen and Prince Hachiko were the same person. Also, between the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|qid=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}} ({{ill|lt=700|Q23494|qid=Q23494|1=700}}), when {{ill|lt=Iwafune no Ki|Q11587922|qid=Q11587922|1=Iwafuke Palisade}} was repaired for the third timeFrom the entry for February 19, year 4 of Emperor Monmu (700), in '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'., and the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|qid=Q23511|1=708}}), when {{ill|lt=Dewa District|Q11395804|qid=Q11395804|1=Ideha District}} was establishedFrom the entry for September 28, Wadō 1 (708), in 'Shoku Nihongi'., subjugation campaigns north of {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|qid=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}} were carried out; it is thought that climbing the {{ill|lt=Dewa Sanzan|Q1155726|qid=Q1155726|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} would not have been possible before campaigns against the {{ill|lt=Emishi|Q932574|qid=Q932574|1=Emishi}} in Dewa beganEdited by Yamagata Prefecture, 'Yamagata Prefecture Report on Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments, Fascicle 11: Dewa Sanzan' (Meicho Shuppan, June 1974; reprint of 1940 edition). In the 10th month decree of {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|qid=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 4 ({{ill|lt=773|Q31131|qid=Q31131|1=773}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinshō Gecchokufu-shō|Q11502312|qid=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}},' two households of shrine-endowment were assigned to the Gassan deity; this is the first appearance of the Gassan deity in historical records. In the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},' articles about the Gassan deity appear repeatedly, including promotions in {{ill|lt=deity rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. Below are the entries in chronological order: * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|qid=Q25041|1=864}}), 2nd month 5th day: the {{ill|lt=Dewa Province|Q876032|qid=Q876032|1=Dewa Province}} deity Gassan no Kami, Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Merit Sixth Class, is promoted to {{ill|lt=Junior Third Rank|Q11071123|qid=Q11071123|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * Jōgan 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|qid=Q25061|1=868}}), 4th month 15th day: report from Dewa Province—six {{ill|lt=stone arrowheads|Q11587274|qid=Q11587274|1=stone arrowhead}} fell like rain before the two shrines of Gassan and Ōmonoimi in {{ill|lt=Akumi District|Q1155592|qid=Q1155592|1=Akumi District, Yamagata}}. * Jōgan 18 ({{ill|lt=876|Q23815|qid=Q23815|1=876}}), 8th month 2nd day: Gassan no Kami of Dewa Province, Junior Third Rank Merit Sixth Class, is promoted to {{ill|lt=Senior Third Rank|Q11123261|qid=Q11123261|1=Senior Third Rank}}. * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|qid=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|qid=Q23910|1=878}}), 7th month 10th day: two additional shrine-endowment households each are granted to Ōmonoimi no Kami (Senior Third Rank Merit Fifth Class) and Gassan no Kami (Senior Third Rank Merit Sixth Class) of Dewa Province. * Gangyō 2 (878), 8th month 4th day: Ōmonoimi no Kami is promoted from Merit Fifth to Merit Third Class; Gassan no Kami from Merit Sixth to Merit Fourth Class. * Gangyō 4 ({{ill|lt=880|Q23618|qid=Q23618|1=880}}), 2nd month 27th day: both Gassan no Kami (Senior Third Rank Merit Fourth Class) and Ōmonoimi no Kami (Senior Third Rank Merit Third Class) are promoted to {{ill|lt=Junior Second Rank|Q11488721|qid=Q11488721|1=Junior Second Rank}}. * {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|qid=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 1 ({{ill|lt=885|Q24894|qid=Q24894|1=885}}), 11th month 21st day: in the previous 6th month 21st day, stone arrowhead rain fell in {{ill|lt=Akita Castle|Q11595283|qid=Q11595283|1=Akita Castle}} and Nishihama of Akumi District in Dewa Province; according to the {{ill|lt=Onmyōryō|Q11658260|qid=Q11658260|1=Yin-Yang Bureau}}, this was an omen of conspiracy and warfare. According to the {{ill|lt=Jingikan|Q2264720|qid=Q2264720|1=Department of Divinities}}, it was a curse due to disrespect toward Ōmonoimi no Kami and Gassan no Kami of Akumi District and {{ill|lt=Yuzusano-me no Kami|Q11577650|qid=Q11577650|lt=Yuzusano-me no Kami|1=Yuzusame Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Tagawa District (Uzen Province)|Q11576762|qid=Q11576762|lt=Tagawa District|1=Tagawa district}}. Therefore an imperial order was issued to the provincial governor to reverently worship the deities and maintain strict guard. In {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it was listed as a Myōjin Taisha in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' In the same '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}},' “Shuzei-shiki,” 2,000 bundles were granted by the state as ritual expenses for Gassan no Kami and Ōmonoimi no Kami. As above, in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}' and '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|1=UNKNOWN}},' the deity of Gassan (a mountain of Tagawa District) is treated as a deity of Akumi District; this is thought to be because at that stage, shrine buildings were maintained and worship conducted in Akumi District near the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|qid=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}}, together with {{ill|lt=Ōmonoimi Shrine|Q11173679|qid=Q11173679|lt=Ōmonoimi Shrine|1=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Chōkai|Q713826|qid=Q713826|1=Mount Chōkai}}. The exact period is unclear, but likely from the middle Heian period until the Meiji {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Gassan functioned as a Shugendō training ground as part of Dewa Sanzan. Although no materials confirming mountain opening can be found up to the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, materials requiring consideration first appear in the Heian period. This religious power of Dewa Sanzan gradually flourished into a major power that could be called a great territorial domain. Consequently, from the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|qid=Q204023|lt=Warring States period|1=Sengoku period}} to the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|qid=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it faced intervention by warlords such as the {{ill|lt=Daihōji clan|Q11434351|qid=Q11434351|lt=Mutō clan|1=Daihōji clan}}, {{ill|lt=Uesugi clan|Q1150058|qid=Q1150058|1=Uesugi clan}}, and {{ill|lt=Mogami clan|Q524625|qid=Q524625|1=Mogami clan}}, who sought to incorporate it into their own power. In particular, {{ill|lt=Mogami Yoshiaki|Q839377|qid=Q839377|1=Mogami Yoshiaki}} repeatedly crossed Gassan when invading the {{ill|lt=Shōnai Plain|Q11485808|qid=Q11485808|1=Shōnai Plain}}, and soldiers ravaged the Muro, looted Buddhist images and sacred treasures, and so on. For this reason, at Mount Haguro, Buddhist images and sacred treasures were hidden in a cave at Nozoki west of Gassan, and to keep the secret, management was entrusted as a one-son hereditary duty to Jūyōbō, a married practitioner. On the other hand, after the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|qid=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}}, Mogami Yoshiaki, who controlled {{ill|lt=Shōnai region|Q11485804|qid=Q11485804|lt=Shōnai|1=Shōnai region}} and {{ill|lt=Yuri District|Q8061492|qid=Q8061492|lt=Yuri|1=Yuri District, Akita}}, repaired and rebuilt Haguro and Gassan to win over Dewa Sanzan. One such effort was repair of this shrine’s main sanctuary in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1601|Q6725|qid=Q6725|1=1601}}). Even in the Edo period, Dewa Sanzan drew people’s faith as a place of Shugendō. In {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1689|Q7674|qid=Q7674|1=1689}}), the haikai master {{ill|lt=Matsuo Bashō|Q5676|qid=Q5676|1=Matsuo Bashō}} visited Dewa Sanzan and worshipped atop Gassan on the 6th day of the 6th month'Oku no Hosomichi' gives the date as June 8, Genroku 2 (1689), but '{{ill|lt=Sora Tabi Nikki|Q1101487|qid=Q1101487|1=Sora's Diary}}' by {{ill|lt=Kawai Sora|Q11141924|qid=Q11141924|1=Kawai Sora}}, who accompanied Bashō and recorded the journey, gives June 6; this is considered correct.. In his travelogue '{{ill|lt=Oku no Hosomichi|Q1287971|qid=Q1287971|1=Oku no Hosomichi}},' he left the verse: “Kumo no mine / ikutsu kuzurete / tsuki no yama” (“How many cloud-peaks collapse—Moon Mountain”).A monument of this verse now stands near the summit on the Ubasawa route. When the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri Order|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|lt=separation order|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} was issued in Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}}), Dewa Sanzan faced an intense wave of {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|qid=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}. Some {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buddhist monks|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=yamabushi|Q2309838|qid=Q2309838|1=Yamabushi}} returned to lay status and became Shinto priests, while others attempted suicide in grief over Buddhist decline. Many precious Buddhist images, ritual implements, and sutra scrolls were lost through destruction or scattered after being sold. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), an edict abolishing Shugendō sects was issued, and it seemed Shugendō in Dewa Sanzan might cease; later, however, surviving yamabushi preserved its lineage through their efforts. Amid such confusion, separation of Shinto and Buddhism in Dewa Sanzan advanced, and on August 31, Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}}), this shrine was ranked as Kokuhei Chūsha under the modern shrine ranking system. It was further promoted to Kanpei Chūsha on April 22, Meiji 18 ({{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|qid=Q7821|1=1885}}), and to Kanpei Taisha on January 4, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|qid=Q2083|1=1914}}). After the end of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=World War II|1=UNKNOWN}}, when the modern shrine rank system was abolished, this shrine became in {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|qid=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23) a {{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the umbrella of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|qid=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), as a religious corporation combining this shrine with {{ill|lt=Dewa Shrine|Q11395802|qid=Q11395802|1=Dewa Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Yudonosan Shrine|Q11563607|qid=Q11563607|1=Yudonosan Shrine}}, '''Dewa Sanzan Shrine''' ('''Sanjin Gōsaiden''') was established on Mount Haguro. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === *{{ill|lt=Kuroita Katsumi|Q3894478|qid=Q3894478|1=Katsumi Kuroita}}, edited by Kokushi Taikei Editorial Association, '{{ill|lt=Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|qid=Q10926780|lt=Kokushi Taikei|1=Kokushi Taikei}} Vol. 27: {{ill|lt=Shinshō Gecchokufu-shō|Q11502312|qid=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}, {{ill|lt=Hōsō Ruirin|Q11555203|qid=Q11555203|1=Hōsō Ruirin}}, {{ill|lt=Ruijū Fusen-shō|Q11665188|qid=Q11665188|1=Ruijufusenshō}}, {{ill|lt=Zoku Sajō-shō|Q11607072|qid=Q11607072|1=Zoku Sajōshō}}, {{ill|lt=Betsujū Fusen-shō|Q11396822|qid=Q11396822|1=Besshūfusenshō}}' ({{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|qid=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}, January 1965) *Kuroita Katsumi, edited by Kokushi Taikei Editorial Association, 'Kokushi Taikei Vol. 4 {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, April 1966) *Kuroita Katsumi, edited by Kokushi Taikei Editorial Association, 'Kokushi Taikei Vol. 2 {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, September 1966) *{{ill|lt=Yasuaki Togawa (folklorist)|Q11496356|qid=Q11496356|lt=Yasuaki Togawa|1=Anshō Togawa}}, 'Dewa Sanzan: History and Culture' (Ikubundō, August 1973) *Edited by Yamagata Prefecture, 'Yamagata Prefecture Report on Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments, Fascicle 11: Dewa Sanzan' (Meicho Shuppan, June 1974; reprint of 1940 edition) *Edited by Yamagata Prefecture, 'Yamagata Prefecture Report on Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments, Fascicle 12: Dewa Sanzan' (Meicho Shuppan, June 1974; reprint of 1941 edition) *Edited by National Shrine Directory Publication Society Historical Center, 'National Shrine Directory Vol. 1' (National Shrine Directory Publication Society Historical Center, July 1977) *Edited by Shintō Taikei Editorial Association, '{{ill|lt=Shintō Taikei|Q11590923|qid=Q11590923|1=Shintō Taikei}} Shrine Section 32: Dewa Sanzan' (Shintō Taikei Editorial Association, August 1982) *Edited by {{ill|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Sites 12, Tōhoku and Hokkaidō' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, June 1984) *Annotated by Yasuo Hagiwara, 'Oku no Hosomichi, with Sora Tabi Nikki and Okunodōchū Kanmoshō' ({{ill|lt=Iwanami Shoten|Q1331427|qid=Q1331427|1=Iwanami Shoten}}, December 1991) ===Related items=== {{commonscat|Gassan-jinja}} **{{ill|lt=Iwanesawa Sanzan Shrine|Q11475213|qid=Q11475213|1=Iwaneaswa Sanzan Shrine}} ===External links=== * [http://www.dewasanzan.jp/ Dewa Sanzan Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{近代社格制度}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Gassan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Gassan Shrine]][[Category:ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shonai Town]] [[Category:History of Shonai Region]]
